name: Reopen PTX Prefetch Handoff On Top Of The New Export Base

summary.name: Reopen PTX Prefetch Handoff On Top Of The New Export Base
summary.hypothesis: Round 8/17 closed the deeper row-pair export cleanup, but round 7/17 established a much better accepted base at 24.84582424 ms by simplifying the PTX export helper. That materially changes the ranking of the remaining families. The earlier prefetch-handoff experiment was negative on the older base, but it also drove long-scoreboard down much harder than the grouping-window attempt. Now that export overhead has been reduced, the next best move is to re-open the PTX prefetch family with a narrower retime on top of the new accepted export base instead of digging deeper into export cleanup again.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Copy-pipeline handoff timing and future-tile refill cadence in the PTX hot-band steady-state loop.
summary.risk: Medium. The family already had one losing attempt, so this should be a narrower retime than round 4/17, but it now sits on a cleaner export baseline that may change the tradeoff.
summary.stop_condition: Stop this reopened family if one bounded retime still fails to beat 24.84582424 ms or merely trades scoreboard for barrier again.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
